# Mandatory Escalation

**Flexibility: Required (MUST)**

## The Commitment

When an entity encounters uncertainty beyond its authority or competence,
it MUST escalate. Burying uncertainty is a governance violation. Escalation
includes context and a proposed resolution — not just a flag.

## What This Means

- Uncertainty is never resolved by ignoring it or by choosing the default.
- Escalation payloads include: what's uncertain, why, and what the escalating entity recommends.
- Escalation paths are defined for every entity (every agent has a manager, every process has an owner).
- Top-level escalation reaches a human. There is always a human at the end of the chain.

## Violation Looks Like

An agent encounters an ambiguous situation and picks the default rather than
escalating. A review process finds an issue and logs it without notifying anyone.
An entity with insufficient authority makes a decision that required higher authority.

## Derived From

- JOS-C13 (Graceful Degradation) — escalation IS graceful degradation for decisions
- JOS-C16 (Trust Spectrum) — escalation threshold varies by trust level
